categories.account.name: Account
categories.ai.name: AI
categories.analysis.name: Analysis
categories.condition.name: Condition
categories.data.name: Data
categories.display.name: Display
categories.infra.description: Workflow entry point and broker connection
categories.infra.name: Infrastructure
categories.market.name: Market
categories.messaging.description: Telegram, Slack and other message notifications
categories.messaging.name: Messaging
categories.order.name: Order
categories.risk.name: Risk
categories.schedule.name: Schedule
categories.system.name: System
nodes.AccountNode.description: Retrieves holdings, cash balance, and position details as a one-time REST API snapshot. Use when you need current portfolio state without live updates. Outputs held_symbols (list of current positions), balance (available cash and total evaluation), and positions (per-symbol quantity, average cost, unrealized P&L). Requires an upstream BrokerNode for authentication. For real-time updates, use RealAccountNode instead. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.AccountNode.name: Account
nodes.AIAgentNode.description: LLM-based AI agent that uses workflow nodes as tools for data analysis and decision making. Connect an LLMModelNode via ai_model edge for LLM access, and connect other nodes (MarketDataNode, AccountNode, etc.) via tool edges to register them as callable tools. Configure system_prompt for role definition, user_prompt for task instructions (supports {{ }} expressions), and output_format (text, json, or structured with schema validation). Includes role presets: risk_manager, technical_analyst, news_analyst, strategist. Built-in safety: 60-second minimum cooldown between executions, max tool call limits, and token usage tracking. Stateless — no conversation memory between executions. Cannot connect directly to real-time nodes without ThrottleNode.
nodes.AIAgentNode.name: AI Agent
nodes.AggregateNode.description: Collects individual items back into a single list or computes an aggregate value. Use with SplitNode to gather per-item results into one array. Supports multiple modes: collect (gather all), filter (by field condition), sum, avg, min, max, count, first, and last. Outputs the collected array, computed value, and item count. Typical connection: SplitNode → [processing nodes] → AggregateNode.
nodes.AggregateNode.name: Aggregate
nodes.IfNode.description: Branches workflow execution based on a condition comparison (if/else). Evaluates left value against right value using one of 12 operators: ==, !=, >, >=, <, <=, in, not_in, contains, not_contains, is_empty, is_not_empty. Outputs to 'true' port when condition passes, 'false' port when it fails, plus a boolean 'result' port. Inactive branches and all their downstream nodes are automatically skipped (cascading skip). Connect downstream nodes using from_port 'true' or 'false' in edges. Typical usage: check account balance before placing orders, or route based on market conditions.
nodes.IfNode.name: If Branch
nodes.AlertNode.description: Sends alert notifications through email, SMS, or webhook channels. Configure message templates with {{ }} expressions for dynamic content from upstream nodes. Use for price alerts, order fill notifications, or error warnings.
nodes.AlertNode.name: Alert
nodes.BacktestEngineNode.description: Runs historical backtests using trading signals and OHLCV data to evaluate strategy performance. Configure data source through items field with from/extract mapping (date, open, high, low, close, volume, symbol, signal, side). Supports five position sizing methods: equal_weight, kelly, fixed_percent, fixed_amount, and atr_based. Includes stop-loss, take-profit, trailing stop, and time stop exit rules. Set initial capital, commission rate, slippage, and risk-free rate. Outputs equity_curve, individual trades, performance metrics (Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, win rate, etc.), and summary. Typical connection: HistoricalDataNode → ConditionNode → BacktestEngineNode → LineChartNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.BacktestEngineNode.name: Backtest Engine
nodes.BarChartNode.description: Renders a bar chart on the workflow canvas for comparing discrete data points. Configure x-axis categories and y-axis values. Use for visualizing performance metrics, symbol rankings, or portfolio distributions. Outputs a 'rendered' signal when display is complete. Typical connection: BacktestEngineNode.metrics → BarChartNode.
nodes.BarChartNode.name: Bar Chart
nodes.BenchmarkCompareNode.description: Compares multiple backtest results against each other or a benchmark for strategy evaluation. Bind strategy results from multiple BacktestEngineNodes, configure date/equity/name field mappings, and select a ranking metric. Supports strategy vs strategy and strategy vs Buy&Hold comparisons. Outputs comparison metrics, outperformance analysis, and strategy rankings. Typical connection: BacktestEngineNode(A) + BacktestEngineNode(B) → BenchmarkCompareNode → MultiLineChartNode.
nodes.BenchmarkCompareNode.name: Benchmark Compare
nodes.BrokerNode.description: Establishes an LS Securities OpenAPI connection for trading. Acts as the authentication gateway — downstream account, market data, and order nodes automatically inherit this broker connection through DAG traversal. Requires credential_id referencing broker credentials (appkey + appsecret). Choose OverseasStockBrokerNode for stocks (NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX) or OverseasFuturesBrokerNode for futures (CME, EUREX, SGX, HKEX). Typical connection: StartNode → BrokerNode → AccountNode / MarketDataNode / NewOrderNode.
nodes.BrokerNode.name: Broker
nodes.CancelOrderNode.description: Cancels a pending (unfilled) order by its original order ID. Requires the original_order_id from OpenOrdersNode or RealOrderEventNode. Outputs cancel result and cancelled order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval to prevent API abuse. Typical connection: OpenOrdersNode → CancelOrderNode, or RealOrderEventNode → CancelOrderNode.
nodes.CancelOrderNode.name: Cancel Order
nodes.CandlestickChartNode.description: Renders an OHLCV candlestick chart on the workflow canvas. Map date, open, high, low, close, and volume fields from historical data. Supports optional signal and side overlays for visualizing buy/sell points on the chart. Outputs a 'rendered' signal when display is complete. Typical connection: HistoricalDataNode → CandlestickChartNode, or BacktestEngineNode → CandlestickChartNode.
nodes.CandlestickChartNode.name: Candlestick Chart
nodes.ConditionNode.description: Evaluates trading conditions using one of 67 community strategy plugins (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Ichimoku, ZScore, TurtleBreakout, MagicFormula, SupportResistanceLevels, etc.). Feed OHLCV or price data through the items field with from/extract mapping, and select a plugin with optional parameters. Outputs is_condition_met (boolean), passed_symbols, failed_symbols, and calculated indicator values. Auto-iterates over input symbol arrays. Bind positions field for sell-side conditions (profit target, stop loss). Typical connection: HistoricalDataNode → ConditionNode → LogicNode or → NewOrderNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.ConditionNode.name: Condition
nodes.CurrencyRateNode.description: Fetches real-time currency exchange rates based on European Central Bank (ECB) data. No broker connection or API key required. Configure a base currency and target currencies to get conversion rates. Includes a convenience KRW rate output port for Korean investors tracking USD/KRW. Built-in resilience with retry on timeout and rate-limit errors, plus fallback to open.er-api.com. Typical connection: CurrencyRateNode → ConditionNode (for FX-based signals) or → FieldMappingNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.CurrencyRateNode.name: Currency Rate
nodes.ErrorHandlerNode.description: Handles errors from upstream nodes and executes recovery logic. Routes error data for logging, alerting, or fallback processing. Use to build resilient workflows that gracefully handle API failures, timeout errors, or invalid data.
nodes.ErrorHandlerNode.name: Error Handler
nodes.EventHandlerNode.description: Processes order lifecycle events including filled, rejected, and cancelled notifications. Routes each event type to appropriate downstream logic for post-trade processing, alert notifications, or order retry workflows.
nodes.EventHandlerNode.name: Event Handler
nodes.FearGreedIndexNode.description: Fetches the CNN Fear & Greed Index, a market sentiment indicator ranging from 0 (Extreme Fear) to 100 (Extreme Greed). No broker connection or API key required. Outputs the current index value (0-100), label (Extreme Fear / Fear / Neutral / Greed / Extreme Greed), and previous day's close value. Built-in resilience with retry on API failures. Typical connection: FearGreedIndexNode → ConditionNode (for sentiment-based trading signals) or → SummaryDisplayNode.
nodes.FearGreedIndexNode.name: Fear & Greed Index
nodes.FundamentalDataNode.description: Fetches company financial data via Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API. Supports four data types: company profile, income statement, balance sheet, and key metrics. Configure period (annual/quarter) and limit (number of periods to fetch). Requires FMP API credentials. Outputs financial data array and query summary. Typical connection: WatchlistNode → FundamentalDataNode → ConditionNode (for fundamental screening) or → TableDisplayNode.
nodes.FundamentalDataNode.name: Fundamental Data (FMP)
nodes.FieldMappingNode.description: Renames field names in data objects to a standard format. Use to normalize external API responses so downstream nodes can access data with consistent field names (e.g., rename 'adj_close' to 'close', 'vol' to 'volume'). Configure mappings as from/to pairs. Outputs mapped_data with renamed fields. Set preserve_unmapped=true to keep original fields alongside renamed ones. Note: this node only renames fields — it does not generate or transform data values. Typical connection: HTTPRequestNode → FieldMappingNode → ConditionNode.
nodes.FieldMappingNode.name: Field Mapping
nodes.FileReaderNode.description: Reads PDF, TXT, CSV, JSON, MD, DOCX, and XLSX files and converts them to text and structured data. Supports batch processing of multiple files via file paths or base64 data input. The node handles parsing only — file sourcing (uploads, S3, HTTP, etc.) is handled externally. PDF: page range selection, table extraction support. CSV: configurable delimiter and header. XLSX: sheet selection. Security: only /app/data/ paths allowed, file size limits enforced. Outputs text array, data array, and metadata array (auto-iterate compatible). Typical connection: HTTPRequestNode → FileReaderNode → AIAgentNode (document analysis) or → ConditionNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.FileReaderNode.name: File Reader
nodes.HTTPRequestNode.description: Makes external REST API calls with support for GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE methods. Configure URL (supports {{ }} expressions for dynamic values), headers, query parameters, and JSON request body. Supports credential-based authentication (Bearer token, header key, basic auth, or query parameter). Built-in resilience with retry on server errors (5xx), rate limits (429), and network failures. Outputs response data, HTTP status code, response headers, and response time in milliseconds. Must use ThrottleNode when connected to real-time data sources. Typical connection: ThrottleNode → HTTPRequestNode → FieldMappingNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.HTTPRequestNode.name: HTTP Request
nodes.HistoricalDataNode.description: Fetches historical OHLCV (Open, High, Low, Close, Volume) chart data over a specified date range. Supports multiple bar intervals: 1-minute, 5-minute, 15-minute, 1-hour, daily, weekly, and monthly. Enable 'adjust' for split/dividend-adjusted prices (recommended for backtesting). Auto-iterates when previous node outputs multiple symbols. Requires an upstream BrokerNode for authentication. Typical connection: WatchlistNode → HistoricalDataNode → ConditionNode or → BacktestEngineNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.HistoricalDataNode.name: Historical Chart
nodes.KoreaStockAccountNode.description: Fetches Korea stock holdings, deposit balance, and P&L as a one-time REST API snapshot. Outputs held_symbols, balance (deposit, total assets, D+2 deposit), and positions (per-symbol quantity, average price, evaluated amount, P&L ratio). Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode. Use KoreaStockOpenOrdersNode for open orders. Use KoreaStockRealAccountNode for live updates. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.KoreaStockAccountNode.name: Korea Stock Account
nodes.KoreaStockBrokerNode.description: Sets up an LS Securities OpenAPI connection for Korea stock trading (KOSPI, KOSDAQ). Acts as the authentication gateway — downstream account, market data, and order nodes automatically inherit the broker connection via DAG traversal. References broker_ls_korea_stock credentials via credential_id. Korea stocks are live-trading only (paper trading not supported). Typical connection: StartNode → KoreaStockBrokerNode → KoreaStockAccountNode / KoreaStockMarketDataNode / KoreaStockNewOrderNode.
nodes.KoreaStockBrokerNode.name: Korea Stock Broker
nodes.KoreaStockCancelOrderNode.descript: Cancels a pending Korea stock order by original order ID. Requires original_order_id from KoreaStockOpenOrdersNode or KoreaStockRealOrderEventNode. Outputs cancel result and cancelled order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval. Typical connection: KoreaStockOpenOrdersNode → KoreaStockCancelOrderNode.
nodes.KoreaStockCancelOrderNode.name: Korea Stock Cancel Order
nodes.KoreaStockFundamentalNode.descript: Fetches fundamental data for a Korea stock symbol including PER, EPS, PBR, market cap, 52-week high/low, and sector. Based on t1102 API. Auto-iterates when the previous node outputs multiple symbols. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode. Typical connection: WatchlistNode → KoreaStockFundamentalNode → ConditionNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.KoreaStockFundamentalNode.name: Korea Stock Fundamentals
nodes.KoreaStockHistoricalDataNode.descr: Fetches historical OHLCV (Open, High, Low, Close, Volume) data for Korea stocks. Supports daily, weekly, and monthly intervals. Enable adjusted prices (recommended for backtesting). Auto-iterates when the previous node outputs multiple symbols. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode. Typical connection: WatchlistNode → KoreaStockHistoricalDataNode → ConditionNode or → BacktestEngineNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.KoreaStockHistoricalDataNode.name: Korea Stock Historical Data
nodes.KoreaStockMarketDataNode.descripti: Fetches current price, volume, change rate, and market cap for a Korea stock symbol as a one-time REST API snapshot. Auto-iterates when the previous node outputs an array of symbols. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode. For continuous price updates, use KoreaStockRealMarketDataNode instead. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.KoreaStockMarketDataNode.name: Korea Stock Current Price
nodes.KoreaStockModifyOrderNode.descript: Modifies the price or quantity of a pending Korea stock order. Requires original_order_id from KoreaStockOpenOrdersNode or KoreaStockRealOrderEventNode. Specify new_price and/or new_quantity. Outputs modify result and modified order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval. Typical connection: KoreaStockOpenOrdersNode → KoreaStockModifyOrderNode.
nodes.KoreaStockModifyOrderNode.name: Korea Stock Modify Order
nodes.KoreaStockNewOrderNode.description: Executes Korea stock buy/sell orders via LS Securities API. Supports limit, market, and conditional limit order types. Auto-iterates when receiving multiple symbols from ConditionNode or PositionSizingNode. Built-in 5-second rate limit and duplicate order prevention. Retry disabled by default to prevent double orders. Typical connection: ConditionNode → PositionSizingNode → KoreaStockNewOrderNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.KoreaStockNewOrderNode.name: Korea Stock New Order
nodes.KoreaStockOpenOrdersNode.descripti: Fetches the list of pending (unfilled) Korea stock orders as a one-time snapshot. Use to check which orders are waiting to be filled, or pass results to KoreaStockModifyOrderNode/KoreaStockCancelOrderNode. Outputs open_orders list (order ID, symbol, side, quantity, price) and count. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.KoreaStockOpenOrdersNode.name: Korea Stock Open Orders
nodes.KoreaStockRealAccountNode.descript: Provides real-time Korea stock holdings, balance, open orders, and P&L. Configure commission rate for accurate P&L calculations. Tax rate is automatically determined by market (KOSPI/KOSDAQ). Outputs held_symbols, balance, open_orders, and positions. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode.
nodes.KoreaStockRealAccountNode.name: Korea Stock Real Account
nodes.KoreaStockRealMarketDataNode.descr: Receives real-time Korea stock trade and quote data via WebSocket. Provides live KOSPI (S3_) and KOSDAQ (K3_) market data. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode. Recommended to use with ThrottleNode to prevent excessive downstream execution.
nodes.KoreaStockRealMarketDataNode.name: Korea Stock Real Market Data
nodes.KoreaStockRealOrderEventNode.descr: Receives real-time Korea stock order events: acceptance, fill, modification, cancellation, and rejection. Routes SC0 (accepted), SC1 (filled), SC2 (modified), SC3 (cancelled), SC4 (rejected) events to separate output ports. Event filter allows selecting specific event types. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode.
nodes.KoreaStockRealOrderEventNode.name: Korea Stock Real Order Event
nodes.KoreaStockSymbolQueryNode.descript: Queries all tradable symbols on KOSPI/KOSDAQ markets. Uses t9945 API to fetch symbol names and codes. Filter by market type (All, KOSPI, KOSDAQ). Outputs symbol list and count. Requires an upstream KoreaStockBrokerNode. Typical connection: KoreaStockSymbolQueryNode → ConditionNode or → KoreaStockMarketDataNode.
nodes.KoreaStockSymbolQueryNode.name: Korea Stock Symbols
nodes.LineChartNode.description: Renders a time-series line chart on the workflow canvas. Configure x-axis field (typically date) and y-axis field (price, equity, etc.), with optional signal and side markers for buy/sell visualization. Bind data from HistoricalDataNode, BacktestEngineNode equity curves, or any time-series output. Outputs a 'rendered' signal when display is complete. Typical connection: BacktestEngineNode.equity_curve → LineChartNode.
nodes.LineChartNode.name: Line Chart
nodes.LLMModelNode.description: Provides LLM (Large Language Model) API connection for AI Agent nodes. Acts as the authentication gateway for LLM services — similar to BrokerNode pattern. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google credentials. Configure model ID (e.g., gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929), temperature, max tokens, seed for reproducibility, and streaming mode. Connect to AIAgentNode via ai_model edge type. Does not execute independently — only provides the connection. Typical connection: LLMModelNode →(ai_model edge)→ AIAgentNode.
nodes.LLMModelNode.name: LLM Model
nodes.LogicNode.description: Combines multiple condition results using logical operators to create complex multi-condition strategies. Supports operators: all (AND), any (OR), not, xor, at_least, at_most, exactly, and weighted scoring. Connect outputs from multiple ConditionNodes or other LogicNodes. Each input includes is_condition_met, passed_symbols, and optional weight. Configure threshold for quantitative operators (at_least/at_most/exactly/weighted). Outputs combined result and merged passed_symbols list. Typical connection: ConditionNode(RSI) + ConditionNode(MACD) → LogicNode(all) → NewOrderNode.
nodes.LogicNode.name: Logic
nodes.MarketDataNode.description: Fetches the current price, volume, and market data for a symbol as a one-time REST API snapshot. Use when you need a price check without live streaming. Auto-iterates when the previous node outputs an array of symbols (e.g., from WatchlistNode). Outputs market data including last price, change, volume, bid/ask, and high/low. Requires an upstream BrokerNode for authentication. For continuous price updates, use RealMarketDataNode instead. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.MarketDataNode.name: Current Price
nodes.MarketUniverseNode.description: Retrieves all constituent stocks from a major US index. Available indices: NASDAQ100, SP500 (S&P 500), SP100 (S&P 100), DOW30 (Dow Jones 30). Overseas stocks only — not available for futures. No broker connection required. Outputs a symbol list and count. Use for broad market screening or index-based strategy building. Typical connection: MarketUniverseNode → ScreenerNode → ConditionNode, or MarketUniverseNode → HistoricalDataNode.
nodes.MarketUniverseNode.name: Index Stocks
nodes.ModifyOrderNode.description: Modifies the price or quantity of a pending (unfilled) order. Requires the original_order_id from OpenOrdersNode or RealOrderEventNode. Specify new_price and/or new_quantity to update. Outputs modify result and modified order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval. Typical connection: OpenOrdersNode → ModifyOrderNode.
nodes.ModifyOrderNode.name: Modify Order
nodes.MultiLineChartNode.description: Renders multiple data series on a single line chart for comparison. Use series_key to group data by a field (e.g., symbol or strategy name). Configure x/y fields, optional signal/side markers, sorting, and display limit. Ideal for comparing multiple strategies or tracking several symbols side by side. Outputs a 'rendered' signal when complete. Typical connection: BenchmarkCompareNode → MultiLineChartNode.
nodes.MultiLineChartNode.name: Multi-Line Chart
nodes.NewOrderNode.description: Executes new buy or sell orders via LS Securities broker API. Supports market and limit order types. Auto-iterates when receiving multiple symbols from ConditionNode or PositionSizingNode. Bind order details (symbol, quantity, price) from upstream nodes using {{ }} expressions. Built-in rate limit (5-second minimum interval) and duplicate order prevention. Retry is disabled by default to avoid double orders. Typical connection: ConditionNode → PositionSizingNode → NewOrderNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.NewOrderNode.name: New Order
nodes.OverseasFuturesAccountNode.descrip: Retrieves overseas futures holdings, margin balance, and position details as a one-time REST API snapshot. Outputs held_symbols, balance (margin, maintenance margin, available margin), and positions (per-contract quantity, average cost, unrealized P&L). Requires an upstream OverseasFuturesBrokerNode for authentication. Use OverseasFuturesOpenOrdersNode separately for pending orders. For real-time updates, use OverseasFuturesRealAccountNode instead. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasFuturesAccountNode.name: Futures Account
nodes.OverseasFuturesBrokerNode.descript: Establishes an LS Securities OpenAPI connection for overseas futures trading (CME, EUREX, SGX, HKEX). Acts as the authentication gateway — downstream account, market data, and order nodes automatically inherit this broker connection through DAG traversal. Requires credential_id referencing broker_ls_overseas_futures credentials. Supports paper trading mode for risk-free testing. Typical connection: StartNode → OverseasFuturesBrokerNode → FuturesAccountNode / FuturesMarketDataNode / FuturesNewOrderNode.
nodes.OverseasFuturesBrokerNode.name: Futures Broker
nodes.OverseasFuturesCancelOrderNode.des: Cancels a pending overseas futures order by its original order ID. Requires original_order_id from FuturesOpenOrdersNode or FuturesRealOrderEventNode. Outputs cancel result and cancelled order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval. Typical connection: FuturesOpenOrdersNode → FuturesCancelOrderNode.
Fetches historical OHLCV chart data for overseas futures over a specified date range. Supports bar intervals from 1-minute to monthly. Enable 'adjust' for rollover-adjusted prices (recommended for backtesting). Auto-iterates when previous node outputs multiple symbols. Requires an upstream OverseasFuturesBrokerNode. Typical connection: FuturesSymbolQueryNode → FuturesHistoricalDataNode → ConditionNode or → BacktestEngineNode. Supported exchanges: CME, EUREX, SGX, HKEX. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasFuturesMarketDataNode.desc: Fetches the current price, volume, and market data for a single overseas futures symbol as a one-time REST API call. Auto-iterates when the previous node outputs an array of symbols. Outputs market data including last price, change, volume, and bid/ask. Requires an upstream OverseasFuturesBrokerNode. Supported exchanges: CME, EUREX, SGX, HKEX. For continuous updates, use OverseasFuturesRealMarketDataNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasFuturesMarketDataNode.name: Futures Market Data
nodes.OverseasFuturesModifyOrderNode.des: Modifies the price or quantity of a pending overseas futures order. Requires original_order_id from FuturesOpenOrdersNode or FuturesRealOrderEventNode. Specify new_price and/or new_quantity to update. Outputs modify result and modified order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval. Typical connection: FuturesOpenOrdersNode → FuturesModifyOrderNode.
nodes.OverseasFuturesNewOrderNode.descri: Executes new buy or sell orders for overseas futures via LS Securities API. Supports market and limit order types with expiry_month (YYYYMM) for contract specification. Auto-iterates when receiving multiple symbols. Built-in rate limit (5-second minimum interval) and duplicate order prevention. Retry is disabled by default to avoid double orders. Typical connection: ConditionNode → PositionSizingNode → FuturesNewOrderNode. Supported exchanges: CME, EUREX, SGX, HKEX. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasFuturesNewOrderNode.name: Futures New Order
nodes.OverseasFuturesOpenOrdersNode.desc: Retrieves all pending (unfilled) overseas futures orders as a one-time snapshot. Use to check which futures orders are waiting to be filled, or feed results into FuturesModifyOrderNode or FuturesCancelOrderNode. Outputs open_orders list (order ID, symbol, side, quantity, price) and count. Requires an upstream OverseasFuturesBrokerNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasFuturesOpenOrdersNode.name: Futures Open Orders
nodes.OverseasFuturesRealAccountNode.des: Streams real-time overseas futures holdings, balance, open orders, and P&L via WebSocket with periodic REST sync. Account data updates automatically as futures prices change. Configure sync_interval_sec for REST refresh frequency (10-3600 seconds), and futures_fee_per_contract for per-contract fee calculation (default $7.5). stay_connected=true keeps the stream running continuously; false fetches once and disconnects. Typical connection: FuturesBrokerNode → FuturesRealAccountNode → ThrottleNode → [downstream logic].
Streams real-time price, volume, and quote updates for overseas futures via WebSocket. Every trade tick triggers downstream node execution. Outputs both OHLCV data and full market data (bid/ask, accumulated volume). stay_connected=true keeps the stream running; false gets a single update. Must use ThrottleNode before AI or HTTP nodes to prevent excessive execution. Typical connection: FuturesBrokerNode → FuturesRealMarketDataNode → ThrottleNode → ConditionNode. Supported exchanges: CME, EUREX, SGX, HKEX.
Receives real-time overseas futures order events via WebSocket, including order accepted, filled, modified, cancelled, and rejected notifications (TC1-TC3 protocols). Each event type routes to a dedicated output port for targeted downstream processing. Use event_filter to subscribe to specific event types or 'all'. stay_connected=true keeps listening continuously. Typical connection: FuturesBrokerNode → FuturesRealOrderEventNode → TelegramNode (for fill alerts).
nodes.OverseasFuturesSymbolQueryNode.des: Queries all tradable overseas futures symbols from a selected exchange. Filter by exchange, contract month, and limit results. Outputs a symbol list and count. Use to discover available futures contracts before building trading strategies. Typical connection: FuturesSymbolQueryNode → FuturesMarketDataNode or → FuturesHistoricalDataNode. Supported exchanges: CME, EUREX, SGX, HKEX.
nodes.OverseasStockAccountNode.descripti: Retrieves overseas stock holdings, cash balance, and position details as a one-time REST API snapshot. Outputs held_symbols (list of current positions), balance (available cash, total evaluation, foreign currency), and positions (per-symbol quantity, average cost, unrealized P&L). Requires an upstream OverseasStockBrokerNode for authentication. Use OverseasStockOpenOrdersNode separately for pending orders. For real-time updates, use OverseasStockRealAccountNode instead. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasStockAccountNode.name: Stock Account
nodes.OverseasStockBrokerNode.descriptio: Establishes an LS Securities OpenAPI connection for overseas stock trading (NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX). Acts as the authentication gateway — downstream account, market data, and order nodes automatically inherit this broker connection through DAG traversal. Requires credential_id referencing broker_ls_overseas_stock credentials (appkey + appsecret). Paper trading is not supported for overseas stocks. Typical connection: StartNode → OverseasStockBrokerNode → AccountNode / MarketDataNode / NewOrderNode.
nodes.OverseasStockBrokerNode.name: Stock Broker
nodes.OverseasStockCancelOrderNode.descr: Cancels a pending US stock order by its original order ID. Requires original_order_id from OverseasStockOpenOrdersNode or OverseasStockRealOrderEventNode. Outputs cancel result and cancelled order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval. Typical connection: StockOpenOrdersNode → StockCancelOrderNode, or StockRealOrderEventNode → StockCancelOrderNode.
nodes.OverseasStockCancelOrderNode.name: Stock Cancel Order
nodes.OverseasStockHistoricalDataNode.de: Fetches historical OHLCV (Open, High, Low, Close, Volume) chart data for overseas stocks over a specified date range. Supports bar intervals: 1-minute, 5-minute, 15-minute, 1-hour, daily, weekly, and monthly. Enable 'adjust' for split/dividend-adjusted prices (recommended for backtesting). Auto-iterates when previous node outputs multiple symbols. Requires an upstream OverseasStockBrokerNode. Typical connection: WatchlistNode → StockHistoricalDataNode → ConditionNode or → BacktestEngineNode. Supported exchanges: NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasStockFundamentalNode.descr: Fetches fundamental financial data for overseas stocks via LS Securities API. Provides 16 metrics including PER, EPS, PBR, market capitalization, dividend yield, 52-week high/low, and more. Auto-iterates when previous node outputs multiple symbols. Requires an upstream OverseasStockBrokerNode. Typical connection: WatchlistNode → StockFundamentalNode → ConditionNode (for value-based screening). Supported exchanges: NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasStockFundamentalNode.name: Stock Fundamentals
nodes.OverseasStockMarketDataNode.descri: Fetches the current price, volume, and market data for a single overseas stock symbol as a one-time REST API call. Auto-iterates when the previous node outputs an array of symbols (e.g., from WatchlistNode). Outputs market data including last price, change, volume, bid/ask, and high/low. Requires an upstream OverseasStockBrokerNode. Supported exchanges: NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX. For continuous updates, use OverseasStockRealMarketDataNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasStockMarketDataNode.name: Stock Market Data
nodes.OverseasStockModifyOrderNode.descr: Modifies the price or quantity of a pending US stock order. Requires original_order_id from OverseasStockOpenOrdersNode or OverseasStockRealOrderEventNode. Specify new_price and/or new_quantity, and select price_type. Outputs modify result and modified order ID. Rate-limited to 5-second minimum interval. Typical connection: StockOpenOrdersNode → StockModifyOrderNode.
nodes.OverseasStockModifyOrderNode.name: Stock Modify Order
nodes.OverseasStockNewOrderNode.descript: Executes new buy or sell orders for US stocks via LS Securities API. Supports market and limit order types, plus US-specific price types (LOO, LOC, MOO, MOC). Auto-iterates when receiving multiple symbols from ConditionNode or PositionSizingNode. Bind order details from upstream nodes using {{ }} expressions. Built-in rate limit (5-second minimum interval) and duplicate order prevention. Retry is disabled by default to avoid double orders. Typical connection: ConditionNode → PositionSizingNode → StockNewOrderNode. Supported exchanges: NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasStockNewOrderNode.name: Stock New Order
nodes.OverseasStockOpenOrdersNode.descri: Retrieves all pending (unfilled) overseas stock orders as a one-time snapshot. Use to check which orders are waiting to be filled, or feed results into StockModifyOrderNode or StockCancelOrderNode. Outputs open_orders list (order ID, symbol, side, quantity, price, status) and count. Requires an upstream OverseasStockBrokerNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.OverseasStockOpenOrdersNode.name: Stock Open Orders
nodes.OverseasStockRealAccountNode.descr: Streams real-time overseas stock holdings, balance, open orders, and P&L via WebSocket with periodic REST sync. Account data updates automatically as market prices change. Configure sync_interval_sec for REST refresh frequency (10-3600 seconds), commission_rate, and tax_rate for accurate P&L calculation. stay_connected=true keeps the stream running continuously; false fetches once and disconnects. Typical connection: StockBrokerNode → StockRealAccountNode → ThrottleNode → [downstream logic].
nodes.OverseasStockRealAccountNode.name: Stock Real-Time Account
nodes.OverseasStockRealMarketDataNode.de: Streams real-time price, volume, and quote updates for overseas stocks via WebSocket. Every trade tick triggers downstream node execution. Outputs both OHLCV data and full market data (bid/ask, accumulated volume). stay_connected=true keeps the stream running; false gets a single update. Must use ThrottleNode before AI or HTTP nodes to prevent excessive execution. Real-time data is available for live trading only (not paper trading). Typical connection: StockBrokerNode → StockRealMarketDataNode → ThrottleNode → ConditionNode. Supported exchanges: NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX.
nodes.OverseasStockRealOrderEventNode.de: Receives real-time overseas stock order events via WebSocket, including order accepted, filled, modified, cancelled, and rejected notifications (AS0-AS4 protocols). Each event type routes to a dedicated output port for targeted downstream processing. Use event_filter to subscribe to specific event types or 'all'. stay_connected=true keeps listening continuously. Typical connection: StockBrokerNode → StockRealOrderEventNode → TelegramNode (for fill alerts) or → NewOrderNode (for order-driven logic).
nodes.OverseasStockSymbolQueryNode.descr: Queries all tradable overseas stock symbols from the exchange. Filter by country, exchange, and limit results. Outputs a symbol list and count. Use to discover available stocks before building trading strategies. Typical connection: StockSymbolQueryNode → ScreenerNode or → MarketDataNode. Supported exchanges: NYSE, NASDAQ, AMEX.
nodes.OverseasStockSymbolQueryNode.name: Stock Symbol Query
nodes.PnLCalculatorNode.description: Calculates real-time profit and loss by combining position data with live market prices. Outputs realized P&L, unrealized P&L, and total P&L. Use for monitoring portfolio performance during live trading sessions.
nodes.PnLCalculatorNode.name: P&L Calculator
nodes.PortfolioNode.description: Allocates capital across multiple strategies and manages portfolio-level rebalancing. Supports four allocation methods: equal weight, custom ratios, risk parity, and momentum-based. Configure rebalancing rules (periodic, drift-based, or both) with customizable frequency and drift threshold. Outputs combined equity curve, performance metrics, allocation weights, rebalance orders, and allocated capital per strategy. Integrates with WorkflowRiskTracker for high-water mark and drawdown tracking. Supports hierarchical portfolio construction (Portfolio of Portfolios). Typical connection: multiple BacktestEngineNodes → PortfolioNode.
nodes.PortfolioNode.name: Portfolio
nodes.PositionSizingNode.description: Calculates order quantities based on account balance and selected sizing method. Supports five methods: fixed_percent (percentage of account), fixed_amount (dollar amount), fixed_quantity (exact shares/contracts), kelly (Kelly criterion fraction), and atr_based (ATR volatility-adjusted sizing). Bind balance from AccountNode and market_data from MarketDataNode for accurate calculations. max_percent caps the maximum position size as a safety limit. Auto-iterates for multiple symbols. Outputs order objects (symbol, exchange, quantity, price) ready to feed into NewOrderNode. Typical connection: AccountNode + MarketDataNode → PositionSizingNode → NewOrderNode.
nodes.PositionSizingNode.name: Position Sizing
nodes.RealAccountNode.description: Streams real-time holdings, cash balance, open orders, and P&L via WebSocket with periodic REST sync. Account data updates automatically as market prices change. Configure sync_interval_sec for REST refresh frequency, commission_rate and tax_rate for accurate P&L calculation. stay_connected=true keeps the stream running continuously; false fetches once and disconnects. Requires an upstream BrokerNode. Typical connection: BrokerNode → RealAccountNode → ThrottleNode → [downstream logic].
nodes.RealAccountNode.name: Real Account
nodes.RealMarketDataNode.description: Streams real-time price, volume, and quote updates via WebSocket. Every trade tick triggers downstream node execution. Outputs both OHLCV data and full market data (bid/ask, accumulated volume). stay_connected=true keeps the stream running; false gets a single update. Must use ThrottleNode before AI or HTTP nodes to prevent excessive execution. Requires an upstream BrokerNode. Typical connection: BrokerNode → RealMarketDataNode → ThrottleNode → ConditionNode.
nodes.RealMarketDataNode.name: Real Market Data
nodes.RealOrderEventNode.description: Receives real-time order events via WebSocket, including order accepted, filled, modified, cancelled, and rejected notifications. Each event type routes to a dedicated output port for targeted downstream processing. Use event_filter to subscribe to specific event types or 'all'. stay_connected=true keeps listening continuously. Requires an upstream BrokerNode. Typical connection: BrokerNode → RealOrderEventNode → TelegramNode (for fill alerts) or → NewOrderNode (for order-driven logic).
nodes.RealOrderEventNode.name: Real Order Event
nodes.SQLiteNode.description: Local SQLite database for persistent data storage and querying within workflows. Supports two operation modes: 'Execute Query' for direct SQL with parameterized queries (:name format), and 'Simple' for GUI-based CRUD operations (select, insert, update, delete, upsert). Database files are stored in /app/data/ directory. Outputs query result rows, affected row count, and last insert ID. Built-in resilience with retry on failures. Typical connection: MarketDataNode → SQLiteNode (save) or SQLiteNode (load) → ConditionNode. Can be used as an AI Agent tool.
nodes.SQLiteNode.name: Simple DB
nodes.ScheduleNode.description: Triggers workflow execution on a cron-based schedule. Configure using standard cron expressions (e.g., '*/5 * * * *' for every 5 minutes, '0 9 * * 1-5' for weekday 9 AM). Set timezone for market-appropriate scheduling. max_duration_hours limits the total running time (0.1-720 hours). Outputs trigger signal, last run time, and next scheduled run time. Typical connection: StartNode → ScheduleNode → BrokerNode → [trading logic].
nodes.ScheduleNode.name: Schedule
nodes.ScreenerNode.description: Filters stocks by fundamental and liquidity criteria such as market cap range, minimum trading volume, sector, and exchange. Automatically excludes low-liquidity stocks. Overseas stocks only — use SymbolQueryNode for futures. Configure max_results to limit output size. Outputs a filtered symbol list and count. Typical connection: MarketUniverseNode → ScreenerNode → HistoricalDataNode → ConditionNode.
nodes.ScreenerNode.name: Find by Conditions
nodes.SplitNode.description: Splits an array into individual items so downstream nodes execute once per item. Use when you need per-symbol or per-position processing from a list output. Supports parallel (simultaneous) or sequential execution with configurable delay between items. continue_on_error allows remaining items to process even if one fails. Outputs current item, index (0-based), and total count. Typical connection: AccountNode → SplitNode → NewOrderNode. Pair with AggregateNode to recollect results.
nodes.SplitNode.name: Split
nodes.StartNode.description: Workflow entry point that initiates DAG execution. Every workflow must contain exactly one StartNode. It fires a start signal that triggers all directly connected downstream nodes. No configuration fields or input ports required. Typical connections: StartNode → BrokerNode, StartNode → ScheduleNode, or StartNode → WatchlistNode.
nodes.StartNode.name: Start
nodes.SummaryDisplayNode.description: Displays JSON objects, key-value pairs, or summary text on the workflow canvas. Accepts any data format — arrays, objects, or primitive values. Use for showing strategy summaries, account snapshots, or debugging intermediate node results. Outputs a 'rendered' signal when display is complete. Typical connection: BacktestEngineNode.summary → SummaryDisplayNode, or AccountNode → SummaryDisplayNode.
nodes.SummaryDisplayNode.name: Summary
nodes.ExclusionListNode.description: Manages a list of stocks to exclude from trading. Combine manually entered symbols with dynamic exclusion sources from other nodes (e.g., current positions to prevent duplicate purchases). When input_symbols is connected, the 'filtered' output provides the source list with exclusions removed. Outputs excluded symbols (deduplicated), count, filtered list, and per-symbol exclusion reasons. Order nodes can reference this list to automatically block orders for excluded symbols. Typical connection: AccountNode → ExclusionListNode ← WatchlistNode, then ExclusionListNode.filtered → NewOrderNode.
nodes.ExclusionListNode.name: Exclusion List
nodes.SymbolFilterNode.description: Compares and filters two symbol lists using set operations (difference, intersection, union). Use to find symbols in one list but not another, symbols common to both lists, or combined unique symbols. Common use cases: find watchlist stocks you don't already own, prevent duplicate purchases, or merge multiple symbol sources. Outputs filtered symbols, excluded symbols, and both counts. Typical connection: WatchlistNode + AccountNode → SymbolFilterNode → NewOrderNode.
nodes.SymbolFilterNode.name: Compare & Filter
nodes.SymbolQueryNode.description: Queries all tradable symbols from the exchange. Filter by country, exchange, and limit results. Outputs a symbol list and count. Use to discover available instruments before building trading strategies. Typical connection: SymbolQueryNode → ScreenerNode or → MarketDataNode.
nodes.SymbolQueryNode.name: All Symbols
nodes.TableDisplayNode.description: Displays data in a sortable table/grid format on the workflow canvas. Configure which columns to show, sort order (ascending/descending), and row limit (1-100). Bind data from any upstream node that outputs arrays or objects. Outputs a 'rendered' signal when display is complete. Typical connection: AccountNode → TableDisplayNode, or BacktestEngineNode.trades → TableDisplayNode.
nodes.TableDisplayNode.name: Table
nodes.TelegramNode.description: Sends messages to Telegram chats, groups, or channels via a Telegram bot. Use for order fill notifications, condition trigger alerts, error warnings, or daily strategy summaries. Requires a credential with bot_token (from @BotFather) and chat_id. Message content supports {{ }} expressions for dynamic values from upstream nodes. Built-in resilience with retry on failures. Typical connection: RealOrderEventNode → TelegramNode (fill alerts), or ConditionNode → TelegramNode (signal alerts).
nodes.TelegramNode.name: Telegram Alert
nodes.ThrottleNode.description: Controls data flow frequency to prevent excessive execution of downstream nodes. Place after real-time nodes (RealMarketDataNode, RealAccountNode) to limit how often downstream logic runs. Supports two modes: 'skip' ignores data during cooldown, 'latest' keeps only the newest data and processes it after cooldown expires. Configure interval_sec (0.1-300 seconds) and pass_first to immediately process the first data point. Outputs the throttled data and throttle statistics (skip count, countdown). Typical connection: RealMarketDataNode → ThrottleNode → ConditionNode.
nodes.ThrottleNode.name: Throttle
nodes.TradingHoursFilterNode.description: Gates signal flow to only pass during specified trading hours. Configure start/end times (HH:MM format), timezone, and active days of the week (Monday-Sunday). Signals arriving within trading hours pass through the 'passed' port; outside hours they route to the 'blocked' port. max_wait_hours limits how long the node waits for trading hours before timing out with passed=false. Use to prevent order execution outside market hours. Typical connection: ScheduleNode → TradingHoursFilterNode → BrokerNode → [order logic].
nodes.TradingHoursFilterNode.name: Trading Hours Filter
nodes.WatchlistNode.description: Manages a user-defined list of stock or futures symbols for tracking and trading. Enter symbols manually with exchange and symbol code (e.g., NASDAQ/AAPL, NYSE/MSFT). No broker connection required. Outputs a symbol list array that downstream nodes auto-iterate over. Typical connection: WatchlistNode → MarketDataNode, WatchlistNode → HistoricalDataNode, or WatchlistNode → ConditionNode.
nodes.WatchlistNode.name: My Watchlist
fields.AIAgentNode.system_prompt_help: Defines the AI agent's role and personality. Automatically filled when selecting a preset.
fields.AIAgentNode.user_prompt_help: Instructions for the AI agent. Reference previous node data using {{ nodes.xxx.yyy }} format.
fields.AIAgentNode.preset_help: Selecting a role preset automatically fills the system prompt and output format.
fields.AIAgentNode.max_tool_calls_help: Maximum number of times the AI can call tools in a single execution.
fields.AIAgentNode.tool_selection_help: all: Pass all tools to LLM, semantic: Select relevant tools via vector similarity (useful when 6+ tools)
fields.AIAgentNode.tool_top_k_help: Number of top tools to pass to LLM in semantic search mode.
fields.AggregateNode.filter_field: Field name to filter by (for filter mode)
fields.AggregateNode.filter_field_helper: Only items where this field is true will be collected
fields.AggregateNode.value_field: Field name for calculation (for sum/avg/min/max modes)
fields.AggregateNode.value_field_helper: Specify a field containing numeric values
fields.BacktestEngineNode.items: Configure data source and field mappings. Set source array in 'from' and field mappings in 'extract'.
fields.BacktestEngineNode.items.extract: Field mappings (e.g., close: {{ row.close }}, signal: {{ row.signal }})
Position side (e.g., {{ row.side }}). Values: long, short (for futures)
fields.BenchmarkCompareNode.date_field: Date field name. Enter the column name representing dates in equity_curve.
fields.BenchmarkCompareNode.equity_field: Equity field name. Enter the column name representing equity value in equity_curve.
fields.BenchmarkCompareNode.name_field: Strategy name field. Enter the column name that identifies each strategy.
fields.BenchmarkCompareNode.strategies: List of strategy results to compare. Bind BacktestEngineNode output or equity_curve.
fields.ConditionNode.items: Configure data source and field mappings. Set source array in 'from' and field mappings in 'extract'.
fields.ConditionNode.items.extract: Field mappings (e.g., symbol: {{ row.symbol }}, close: {{ row.close }})
fields.ConditionNode.positions: Current position data. Use to reference held symbols in sell conditions.
fields.CurrencyRateNode.base_currency: Base currency. Rates are quoted against this currency.
fields.CurrencyRateNode.resilience: Retry and fallback settings for API call failures
fields.CurrencyRateNode.target_currencie: Target currencies. Select currencies to get exchange rates against the base currency.
fields.CurrencyRateNode.timeout_seconds: API request timeout (seconds). Default 30s, adjust based on your network conditions.
fields.FieldMappingNode.data: Input data to transform. Bind from HTTPRequestNode response or other node outputs.
fields.FieldMappingNode.mappings: Field name mapping table. Converts source field names (from) to standard field names (to).
fields.FieldMappingNode.mappings.from: Source field name (actual field name from API response)
fields.FieldMappingNode.mappings.to: Standard field name (e.g., close, volume, date)
fields.FieldMappingNode.preserve_unmappe: Whether to keep unmapped fields. If checked, original fields are also included in output.
fields.FileReaderNode.file_paths: Array of file paths to read. Relative to /app/data/. (e.g., ["uploads/a.pdf", "uploads/b.csv"])
fields.FileReaderNode.file_path: Single file path (convenience field). Used when file_paths is not set.
fields.FileReaderNode.file_data_list: Array of base64-encoded file data. Pass file content directly instead of paths.
fields.FileReaderNode.file_data: Single base64 file data (convenience field). Used when file_data_list is not set.
fields.FileReaderNode.file_names: Array of file names. Used for format auto-detection and metadata.
fields.FileReaderNode.file_name: Single file name (convenience field). Used when file_names is not set.
fields.FileReaderNode.format: File format: auto (auto-detect), pdf, txt, csv, json, md, docx, xlsx
fields.FileReaderNode.encoding: Text encoding. Default utf-8. Applied to CSV/TXT/JSON/MD files.
fields.FileReaderNode.pages: PDF page range. e.g., "1-5", "1,3,5", "2-". All pages if not set.
fields.FileReaderNode.extract_tables: Enable PDF table extraction. Uses pdfplumber to convert tables to structured data.
fields.FileReaderNode.delimiter: CSV delimiter. Default comma (,). Use "\t" for tab-separated.
fields.FileReaderNode.has_header: Use first CSV row as header. If checked, outputs dict array; otherwise list array.
fields.FileReaderNode.sheet_name: XLSX sheet name. Reads first sheet if not set.
fields.FileReaderNode.max_file_size_mb: Maximum file size per file (MB). Range 1-50, default 10MB.
fields.FearGreedIndexNode.resilience: Retry and fallback settings for API call failures
fields.FearGreedIndexNode.timeout_second: API request timeout (seconds). Default 30s, adjust based on your network conditions.
fields.FundamentalDataNode.credential_id: FMP API credentials. Register your API key from financialmodelingprep.com.
fields.FundamentalDataNode.data_type: Data type: profile, income_statement, balance_sheet, key_metrics
fields.FundamentalDataNode.period: Period: annual or quarter. Used for financial statements and key metrics.
fields.FundamentalDataNode.limit: Number of periods to fetch (most recent N). Default 1.
fields.FundamentalDataNode.resilience: Retry and fallback settings for API call failures
fields.HTTPRequestNode.body: Request body for POST/PUT/PATCH. Automatically serialized to JSON.
fields.HTTPRequestNode.credential_id: Credential ID for authentication. Supports: http_bearer, http_header, http_basic, http_query
fields.HTTPRequestNode.headers: HTTP headers. Click + to add custom headers (e.g., Content-Type, X-API-Key).
fields.HTTPRequestNode.query_params: URL query parameters. Click + to add key-value pairs.
fields.HTTPRequestNode.resilience: Retry and fallback settings for API call failures
fields.HTTPRequestNode.retry_count: Number of retry attempts on failure. 0 = no retry.
fields.HTTPRequestNode.timeout_seconds: How long to wait for a response (seconds). If no response in this time, it fails. Increase for slow APIs.
fields.HTTPRequestNode.url: Request URL. Supports {{ }} expressions for dynamic values.
fields.KoreaStockFundamentalNode.symbol: Enter the symbol code for fundamental data lookup
Enter directly or bind (fx toggle). 6-digit symbol code.
fields.KoreaStockHistoricalDataNode.adju: Uses prices adjusted for stock splits and dividends. Recommended for backtesting.
fields.KoreaStockHistoricalDataNode.star: e.g., 20240101. Use {{ date.ago(30, format='yyyymmdd') }} for binding
fields.KoreaStockHistoricalDataNode.symb: Enter the symbol code to query
Enter directly or bind (fx toggle). 6-digit symbol code.
fields.KoreaStockMarketDataNode.symbol: Enter the symbol code for current price lookup
fields.KoreaStockMarketDataNode.symbol.h: Enter directly or bind (fx toggle). 6-digit symbol code.
fields.KoreaStockModifyOrderNode.new_pri: New price. Leave empty to keep the original price.
fields.KoreaStockModifyOrderNode.new_qua: New quantity. Leave empty to keep the original quantity.
fields.KoreaStockNewOrderNode.order: Configure order details: symbol, quantity, and price.
fields.KoreaStockNewOrderNode.order.help: Connect to PositionSizingNode or MarketDataNode to auto-fill order information.
fields.KoreaStockNewOrderNode.order_type: Order type. Market orders fill immediately, limit orders execute at your specified price.
fields.KoreaStockNewOrderNode.price_type: Price type. Choose limit, market, or conditional limit.
fields.KoreaStockRealAccountNode.commiss: Korea stock trading commission rate (%). LS Securities default 0.015%
fields.KoreaStockRealAccountNode.market: Market type. Select KOSPI or KOSDAQ. Used for automatic tax rate determination.
fields.KoreaStockRealAccountNode.stay_co:
✅ ON: Continuously receives real-time account data.
❌ OFF: Fetches account data once and disconnects.
fields.KoreaStockRealAccountNode.sync_in: Account sync interval (seconds). e.g., 60 refreshes balance/positions every minute.
fields.KoreaStockRealMarketDataNode.stay:
✅ ON: Continuously receives real-time market data until manually stopped.
❌ OFF: Receives data once and disconnects.
fields.KoreaStockRealMarketDataNode.symb: Symbol code for real-time market data subscription
Enter directly or bind (fx toggle). 6-digit symbol code.
fields.KoreaStockRealOrderEventNode.even: Select which Korea stock order notifications to receive. Choose 'All' to get all order events.
fields.KoreaStockRealOrderEventNode.stay:
✅ ON: Continuously receives order fill/reject/cancel notifications.
❌ OFF: Receives events once and disconnects.
fields.KoreaStockSymbolQueryNode.market: Select the market to query. Choose All, KOSPI, or KOSDAQ.
fields.LLMModelNode.model_help: LLM model ID to use. e.g. gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929, llama3.1
fields.LLMModelNode.seed_help: Using the same seed value produces similar outputs for the same input. Useful for strategy reproducibility.
fields.LogicNode.conditions.help_text:
Configure conditions for logical operation.

Each condition requires:
• is_condition_met: Whether condition is met ({{ nodes.nodeId.result }})
• passed_symbols: Symbols that passed ({{ nodes.nodeId.passed_symbols }})
• weight: Weight value (for weighted operator)

💡 You can connect ConditionNode, LogicNode, or any node with result output.
fields.LogicNode.operator.help_text:
Choose how to combine multiple conditions.
• all: Pass only if ALL conditions pass
• any: Pass if ANY condition passes
• at_least: Pass if N or more conditions pass
• weighted: Sum weighted scores
fields.LogicNode.threshold: Threshold value (for at_least, at_most, exactly, weighted operators)
fields.LogicNode.threshold.help_text:
• at_least/at_most/exactly: Number of conditions required (integer)
  e.g., threshold=2 → at least/most/exactly 2 must pass
• weighted: Score threshold (decimal 0~1)
  e.g., threshold=0.6 → pass if weighted sum ≥ 60%
fields.MarketUniverseNode.universe: Enter the index to fetch. ⚠️ Overseas stocks only. Available values: NASDAQ100, SP500 (S&P 500), SP100 (S&P 100), DOW30 (Dow Jones 30)
fields.OverseasFuturesHistoricalDataNode: Uses adjusted prices reflecting rollovers, etc. Recommended for backtesting.
Enter the futures symbol code to query
Direct input or binding available (fx toggle)
fields.OverseasFuturesMarketDataNode.sym: Enter the futures symbol code for current price lookup
Direct input or binding available (fx toggle)
fields.OverseasFuturesNewOrderNode.order: Configure order details. Enter symbol, quantity, and price.
Connect with PositionSizingNode or MarketDataNode to auto-fill order details.
Order type. Market executes immediately, limit orders at your specified price.
List of orders. Bind MarketDataNode.values to auto-use price data per symbol.
fields.OverseasFuturesRealAccountNode.fu: Overseas futures per-contract fee (USD, one-way). LS Securities default: $7.5
fields.OverseasFuturesRealAccountNode.st:
✅ On: Keep receiving real-time account data.
❌ Off: Get account data once and disconnect.
fields.OverseasFuturesRealAccountNode.sy: Account data refresh interval (seconds). e.g., 60 = refresh balance/positions every minute.
fields.OverseasFuturesRealMarketDataNode:
✅ On: Keep receiving real-time quotes. Stays connected until manually stopped.
❌ Off: Get quotes once and auto-disconnect.
Futures symbol code for real-time market data subscription

fields.OverseasFuturesRealOrderEventNode: Select which overseas futures order events to receive. 'All' receives all order events.

❌ Off: Receive events once and disconnect.
fields.OverseasStockHistoricalDataNode.a: Uses adjusted prices reflecting stock splits, dividends, etc. Recommended for backtesting.
fields.OverseasStockHistoricalDataNode.s: Enter the symbol code to query
Direct input or binding available (fx toggle)
fields.OverseasStockFundamentalNode.symb: Enter the symbol code for fundamental data lookup
Direct input or binding available (fx toggle)
fields.OverseasStockMarketDataNode.symbo: Enter the symbol code for current price lookup
Direct input or binding available (fx toggle)
fields.OverseasStockModifyOrderNode.new_: New price. Leave empty to keep current price.
New quantity. Leave empty to keep current quantity.
fields.OverseasStockNewOrderNode.order: Configure order details. Enter symbol, quantity, and price.
fields.OverseasStockNewOrderNode.order.h: Connect with PositionSizingNode or MarketDataNode to auto-fill order details.
fields.OverseasStockNewOrderNode.order_t: Order type. Market executes immediately, limit orders at your specified price.
fields.OverseasStockNewOrderNode.orders: List of orders. Bind MarketDataNode.values to auto-use price data per symbol.
fields.OverseasStockNewOrderNode.price_t: Price type. LOO/LOC/MOO/MOC are US market special orders.
fields.OverseasStockRealAccountNode.comm: Overseas stock trading commission rate (%). LS Securities default: 0.25%
fields.OverseasStockRealAccountNode.stay:
✅ On: Keep receiving real-time account data.
❌ Off: Get account data once and disconnect.
fields.OverseasStockRealAccountNode.sync: Account data refresh interval (seconds). e.g., 60 = refresh balance/positions every minute.
fields.OverseasStockRealAccountNode.tax_: Overseas stock transaction tax rate (%). US: 0%, HK: 0.1%, JP: 0%
fields.OverseasStockRealMarketDataNode.s:
✅ On: Keep receiving real-time quotes. Stays connected until manually stopped.
❌ Off: Get quotes once and auto-disconnect.
Symbol code for real-time market data subscription
Direct input or binding available (fx toggle)

fields.OverseasStockRealOrderEventNode.e: Select which overseas stock order events to receive. 'All' receives all order events.
fields.OverseasStockRealOrderEventNode.s:
✅ On: Keep receiving order fill/reject/cancel events.
❌ Off: Receive events once and disconnect.
fields.PositionSizingNode.atr_risk_perce:
📉 Account % to risk per ATR move.

ATR measures average price volatility. Position size is calculated to limit loss.

Example: 1.0 → if price drops by 1 ATR, lose only 1% of account.

Recommended: 0.5~2.0%
fields.PositionSizingNode.balance: 💰 Available balance for trading. Enter a number. (e.g., 10000)
fields.PositionSizingNode.fixed_amount:
💰 Fixed amount to invest per symbol. Example: 10000 → always invest $10,000 per symbol.

💡 Binding tip: Connect AccountNode balance. Example: {{ nodes.account.balance }}
fields.PositionSizingNode.fixed_quantity:
📦 Fixed quantity per symbol. Example: 1 → order 1 contract/share for each symbol.

💡 Useful for futures testing or small-scale trading.
fields.PositionSizingNode.kelly_fraction:
🎲 Kelly formula adjustment (0.0~1.0).

Full Kelly (1.0) is highly volatile.

Recommended: 0.25 (Quarter Kelly) / 0.5 (Half Kelly) / 1.0 (Full Kelly, high risk)
fields.PositionSizingNode.market_data: 📈 Current price data per symbol. Bind the output of MarketDataNode.
fields.PositionSizingNode.market_data.he: Having current price data enables more accurate quantity calculation.
fields.PositionSizingNode.max_percent:
📊 [Fixed Percent] Percentage of account to invest per symbol. Example: 10 → invest 10% of account per symbol.

🛡️ [Kelly/ATR] Upper limit for calculated position size.
fields.PositionSizingNode.method: Choose how to determine the investment amount per symbol.
fields.PositionSizingNode.symbol.help_te: Direct input or binding available (fx toggle)
fields.SQLiteNode.columns: Columns to select or insert. Leave empty for all columns (*).
fields.SQLiteNode.columns_help: Enter comma-separated values. Example: symbol, price, volume
fields.SQLiteNode.on_conflict: Conflict column for UPSERT. Executes UPDATE when this column has duplicates.
fields.SQLiteNode.on_conflict_help: Specify a Primary Key or UNIQUE column.
fields.SQLiteNode.operation: Select operation mode. 'Execute Query' for direct SQL, 'Simple' for GUI-based operations.
fields.SQLiteNode.parameters: Parameter values for SQL query. Matches :name in the query.
fields.SQLiteNode.query: SQL query to execute. Bind parameters using :name format. Example: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = :user_id
fields.SQLiteNode.values: Values to insert or update. Enter column names and values.
fields.SQLiteNode.where_clause: WHERE clause condition. Use :name format for parameter binding.
fields.SQLiteNode.where_clause_detailed:
WHERE clause for filtering rows. Use :name format for parameter binding.

Examples:
• symbol = :symbol → Filter by symbol
• price > :min_price → Price condition
• date >= :start_date AND date <= :end_date → Date range
• symbol IN (:sym1, :sym2) → Multiple values
fields.ScheduleNode.max_duration_hours_h: Maximum running time for the schedule loop. Automatically stops when exceeded.
fields.SplitNode.continue_on_error: Continue executing remaining items even if one fails
fields.SplitNode.delay_ms: Delay between items in milliseconds (sequential mode only)
fields.SplitNode.delay_ms_helper: Set appropriate delay to avoid API rate limits
fields.SplitNode.parallel: Execute all items simultaneously. Off = sequential execution.
fields.TelegramNode.bot_token: Telegram bot token. Get it from @BotFather after creating your bot.
fields.TelegramNode.chat_id: Chat ID to receive messages. Works with private chats, groups, and channels.
fields.TelegramNode.credential_id: Telegram credential ID. Select credential containing bot_token and chat_id.
fields.TelegramNode.message: Message content to send. Use {{ }} expressions for dynamic values.
fields.ThrottleNode.interval_sec: Minimum execution interval (seconds). Data within this time is processed according to mode.
fields.ThrottleNode.mode: Select mode. skip: ignore data during cooldown, latest: keep only newest data and execute after cooldown
fields.ThrottleNode.pass_first: Whether to pass the first data immediately
fields.TradingHoursFilterNode.max_wait_h: Maximum time to wait for trading hours. Times out with passed=false when exceeded.
fields.ExclusionListNode.default_reason: Default exclusion reason. Applied when individual symbols have no reason specified.
fields.ExclusionListNode.dynamic_symbols: Connect symbols to exclude dynamically from other nodes. Example: Connect held positions to prevent duplicate purchases.
fields.ExclusionListNode.input_symbols: Source symbol list to filter. When connected, the filtered port outputs symbols with exclusions removed.
fields.ExclusionListNode.symbols: List of symbols to exclude from trading. Enter exchange, symbol code, and optional exclusion reason for each.
fieldNames.BarChartNode.title: Title
fieldNames.CandlestickChartNode.title: Title
fieldNames.LineChartNode.title: Title
fieldNames.MultiLineChartNode.title: Title
fieldNames.SummaryDisplayNode.title: Title
fieldNames.TableDisplayNode.title: Title
outputs.AIAgentNode.response: AI agent response (text or JSON object depending on output format)
outputs.ExclusionListNode.filtered: Source symbols minus excluded symbols (when input_symbols connected)
outputs.ConditionNode.values: Calculated values used for condition (e.g., RSI, MACD)
outputs.CurrencyRateNode.rates: Exchange rate data array [{base, target, rate, timestamp}]
outputs.FileReaderNode.data_list: Structured data array per file (CSV→dict[], JSON→object, XLSX→dict[])
outputs.FileReaderNode.metadata: Metadata array per file [{file_name, format, size_bytes, encoding, ...}]
outputs.FearGreedIndexNode.label: Index label (Extreme Fear / Fear / Neutral / Greed / Extreme Greed)
outputs.FearGreedIndexNode.previous_clos: Previous day's Fear & Greed Index value
outputs.LLMModelNode.connection: LLM connection info (connect to AI Agent node's ai_model port)
outputs.common.error: Error message if an error occurred
ports.fundamental_data_value: Fundamental data (PER, EPS, market cap, etc.)
enums.operator.all: All Match (AND) - Every condition must pass
enums.operator.any: Any Match (OR) - At least one must pass
enums.operator.at_least: At Least N - threshold or more must pass
enums.operator.at_most: At Most N - threshold or fewer must pass
enums.operator.exactly: Exactly N - exactly threshold must pass
enums.operator.not: None Match (NOT) - Pass only if all fail
enums.operator.xor: Exactly One (XOR) - Only one must pass
enum.PositionSizingNode.method.fixed_per: 📊 Fixed Percent - Invest X% of account
enum.PositionSizingNode.method.fixed_qua: 📦 Fixed Quantity - Same quantity per symbol
display_schema.line.x: X-axis value (key name determined by x_field setting)
display_schema.line.y: Y-axis value (key name determined by y_field setting)
display_schema.table.columns: Columns to display (determined by columns setting)
errors.CREDENTIAL_NOT_SET: Credential is not set. Check BrokerNode's credential_id.
resilience.fallback.default_value.descri: Default value to return on failure (JSON)
ui.confirm_delete_database: Are you sure you want to delete '{name}' database? All data will be permanently deleted.
connection_rules.realtime_to_order.reaso: Direct connection from realtime node to order node is blocked. Each tick would trigger an order, potentially causing unintended mass orders.
connection_rules.realtime_to_order.sugge: Place a ThrottleNode between the realtime node and order node to control execution frequency.
connection_rules.realtime_to_ai_agent.re: Direct connection from realtime node to AIAgentNode is blocked. Each tick would trigger an LLM call, potentially causing excessive costs.
connection_rules.realtime_to_ai_agent.su: Place a ThrottleNode between the realtime node and AI Agent to control call frequency.
connection_rules.realtime_to_external_ap: Direct connection from realtime node to external API node is not recommended. Each tick would trigger an external API request, potentially hitting rate limits.
Place a ThrottleNode between the realtime node and external API node to control request frequency.
connection_rules.realtime_to_http.reason: Direct connection from realtime node to HTTPRequestNode is not recommended. Each tick would trigger an HTTP request, potentially hitting external API rate limits.
connection_rules.realtime_to_http.sugges: Place a ThrottleNode between the realtime node and HTTP request node to control request frequency.
fields.BaseOrderNode.rate_limit_interval: Minimum order interval (seconds). Default 5 seconds. The next order will only execute after this time has elapsed since the last order.
fields.BaseOrderNode.rate_limit_action: Action when order interval is not met. skip: silently skip, error: raise error
plugins.TimeSeriesMomentum.name: Time Series Momentum
plugins.TimeSeriesMomentum.description: Determines trading direction based on past returns of individual assets (Moskowitz et al. 2012)
plugins.ConnorsRSI.name: Connors RSI
plugins.ConnorsRSI.description: Combines RSI + Streak RSI + Percentile Rank for short-term overbought/oversold detection (Connors 2008)
plugins.MFI.name: Money Flow Index
plugins.MFI.description: Volume-weighted RSI analyzing money flow in/out
plugins.CoppockCurve.name: Coppock Curve
plugins.CoppockCurve.description: WMA of long-term ROC for market bottom buy signals (Coppock 1962)
plugins.ElderRay.name: Elder Ray
plugins.ElderRay.description: Analyzes buying/selling pressure via Bull/Bear Power (Elder 1993)
plugins.TurtleBreakout.name: Turtle Breakout
plugins.TurtleBreakout.description: Donchian channel breakout with ATR-based risk management (Dennis 1983)
plugins.VolatilityBreakout.name: Volatility Breakout
plugins.VolatilityBreakout.description: Buy when price exceeds previous day range × K factor (Larry Williams)
plugins.SeasonalFilter.name: Seasonal Filter
plugins.SeasonalFilter.description: Sell in May / Halloween effect based timing filter (Bouman & Jacobsen 2002)
plugins.TacticalAssetAllocation.name: Tactical Asset Allocation
plugins.TacticalAssetAllocation.descript: Hold/cash decision based on N-day SMA crossover (Faber 2007)
plugins.MagicFormula.name: Magic Formula
plugins.MagicFormula.description: Selects value stocks by combined ROC + earnings yield ranking (Greenblatt 2005)
plugins.SupportResistanceLevels.name: Support/Resistance Levels
plugins.SupportResistanceLevels.descript: Dynamic S/R level detection from swing highs/lows with cluster zone strength evaluation
plugins.LevelTouch.name: Level Touch
plugins.LevelTouch.description: S/R level touch/breakout/role reversal detection with history tracking (first_touch, role_reversal, cluster_bounce)
events.restart.data_gap_warning: Data gap warning during restore: data may be missing for {gap_sec} seconds since checkpoint
events.restart.workflow_changed: Workflow definition changed, cannot restore (hash mismatch)
